Subparagraph.Text: the particular corporation would hold qualifying voting control in respect of the other corporation if the particular corporation were to own

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: all of the issued and outstanding specified shares of the capital stock of the other corporation that are included in clauses (i)(A) to (D), and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: all of the issued and outstanding shares of the capital stock of the other corporation that are not specified shares and that would be included in clauses (i)(A) to (D) if they were specified shares; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 3(b) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: qualifying voting control in respect of the other corporation is held by, and not less than 90% of the total value and number of all specified shares of the other corporation are owned by,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the particular corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation that is closely related to the particular corporation by reason of paragraph 128(1)(a) of the Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation that is a prescribed corporation in relation to the particular corporation by reason of paragraph (a), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any combination of the corporations referred to in subparagraphs (i) to (iii).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (6) apply as of March 22, 2017. They also apply as of March 23, 2016

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of an election under subsection 150(1) or 156(2) of the Excise Tax Act that is to become effective on a day that is after March 22, 2016 but before March 22, 2017, unless the election is filed on or before March 22, 2016; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of applying paragraphs 4(3)(b) and (c) of the Financial Services and Financial Institutions (GST/HST) Regulations in respect of a supply of a service if the agreement for the supply is entered into after March 22, 2016 but before March 22, 2017 and it is not the case that all or substantially all of the service will be performed before March 22, 2017.

Subsection.Text: The definition eligible capital property in subsection 2(1) of the Streamlined Accounting (GST/HST) Regulations is repealed.
The definition capital asset in subsection 2(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: capital asset of a person means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: property that is, or would be if the person were a taxpayer under the Income Tax Act, capital property of the person within the meaning of that Act, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a supply that was made by the person at any time before January 1, 2017, property that was, or would have been if the person were a taxpayer under the Income Tax Act, eligible capital property of the person within the meaning of that Act as it read at that time; (bien immobilisé)

Subsection.Text: The descriptions of A and B in subsection 2(2) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all consideration (other than consideration referred to in section 167.1 of the Act that is attributable to goodwill of a business) that became due, or was paid without having become due, to the registrant in the threshold period for the reporting period for taxable supplies (other than supplies of financial services, supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the registrant and supplies deemed under subsection 177(1.2) of the Act to be made by the registrant) that are or would be, but for that subsection, made in Canada by the registrant;

is the total of all tax under Division II that became collectible in the threshold period in respect of taxable supplies (other than supplies of financial services, supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the registrant and supplies deemed under subsection 177(1.2) of the Act to be made by the registrant) that are or would be, but for that subsection, made in Canada by the registrant; and

Subsection.Text: The descriptions of A and B in paragraph 2(3)(a) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all consideration (other than consideration referred to in section 167.1 of the Act that is attributable to goodwill of a business) for taxable supplies (other than supplies of financial services and supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the registrant) made by the registrant that became due, or was paid without having become due, to the registrant in the threshold period for the reporting period,

is the total of all tax under Division II that became collectible in the threshold period in respect of taxable supplies (other than supplies of financial services and supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the registrant) made by the registrant, and

Subsection.Text: The descriptions of D and E in paragraph 2(3)(b) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all consideration (other than consideration referred to in section 167.1 of the Act that is attributable to goodwill of a business) for taxable supplies (other than supplies of financial services and supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the associate) made by the associate that became due, or was paid without having become due, to the associate in the particular fiscal year,

is the total of all tax under Division II that became collectible in the particular fiscal year in respect of taxable supplies (other than supplies of financial services and supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the associate) made by the associate, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of supplies made after 2016.
Subsections (2) to (5) come into force or are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2017.
The definition specified property in subsection 15(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: specified property, in respect of a person, means property of the person other than real property and capital assets of the person; (bien déterminé)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition specified supply in subsection 15(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a supply by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the supplier,

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force or are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2017.
The definition specified property in subsection 19(1) of the Regulations is repealed.
Paragraph (a) of the definition designated supply in subsection 19(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a supply by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the supplier,

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs (b) and (c) of the definition specified supply in subsection 19(1) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a supply by way of sale of a capital asset of the registrant that has a fair market value at the time of the supply of at least $10,000,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a supply made by the registrant by way of sale of a capital asset of the registrant if the registrant has claimed, or is entitled to claim, an input tax credit in respect of the last supply to, or importation by, the registrant of the capital asset,

Subsection.Text: The description of B in subparagraph 19(3)(c)(i) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all tax under Division II that became collectible in the fiscal year of the registrant immediately before the particular fiscal year in respect of taxable supplies (other than supplies by way of sale of real property or capital assets of the registrant) made by the registrant, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) come into force or are deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2017.
Subparagraphs (a)(ii) and (iii) of the description of C in subsection 21(1) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: for the particular reporting period or a preceding reporting period of the registrant during which the election was in effect in respect of a supply by way of sale to, importation by, or bringing into a participating province by, the registrant of personal property that is acquired, imported or brought into the participating province by the registrant for use as a capital asset of the registrant and that has a fair market value at the time of the supply or bringing into the province, or a value as determined under section 215 of the Act at the time of the importation, of at least $10,000,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: for the particular reporting period or a preceding reporting period of the registrant during which the election was in effect in respect of an improvement to a capital asset (other than real property) of the registrant, if the registrant has claimed, or is entitled to claim, an input tax credit in respect of the last supply to, or importation by, the registrant of the capital asset,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2017.
Subsection 191(3) of the Excise Act, 2001 is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to give effect to an alternative basis or argument advanced by the Minister under subsection (7).

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 191(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Minister may advance an alternative basis or argument in support of an assessment of a person, or in support of all or any portion of the total amount determined on assessment to be payable or remittable by a person under this Act, at any time after the period otherwise limited by subsection (1) or (2) for making the assessment unless on an appeal under this Act
Section 191 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

If a reassessment of a person is made that gives effect to an alternative basis or argument advanced by the Minister under subsection (7) in support of a particular assessment of the person, the Minister shall not reassess for an amount that is greater than the total amount of the particular assessment.

Subsection (7.1) does not apply to any portion of an amount determined on reassessment that the Minister would be entitled to reassess under this Act at any time after the period otherwise limited by subsection (1) or (2) for making the reassessment if this Act were read without reference to subsection (7).
Subsections (1) to (3) come into force on the day on which this Act receives royal assent except that those subsections do not apply in respect of appeals instituted on or before that day.

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraphs 18(1)(a) and 27(1)(a) to (c) and subsection 50(8), employment is not suitable employment for a claimant if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it arises in consequence of a work stoppage attributable to a labour dispute;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is in the claimant’s usual occupation and is either at a lower rate of earnings or on conditions less favourable than those observed by agreement between employers and employees or, in the absence of any such agreement, than those recognized by good employers; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is not in the claimant’s usual occupation and is either at a lower rate of earnings or on conditions less favourable than those that the claimant might reasonably expect to obtain, having regard to the conditions that the claimant usually obtained in their usual occupation, or would have obtained if they had continued to be so employed.

Subsection.Text: After a lapse of a reasonable interval from the date on which an insured person becomes unemployed, paragraph (4)(c) does not apply to the employment described in that paragraph if it is employment at a rate of earnings not lower and on conditions not less favourable than those observed by agreement between employers and employees or, in the absence of any such agreement, than those recognized by good employers.

Section.Text: Subsection 27(2) of the Act is repealed.
This Division comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Section 19 of the Old Age Security Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Direction by Minister if spouses or common-law partners living apart
Subsection.Text: If an application has been made under subsection (4) for payment of an allowance to a pensioner’s spouse or common-law partner in respect of any payment period, the Minister may, after any investigation of the circumstances that he or she considers necessary, direct that for the purpose of calculating the allowance, the monthly joint income, as defined in subsection 22(1), be determined without taking into account the pensioner’s income for the base calendar year in any case where the Minister is satisfied that the spouse or common-law partner, as a result of circumstances not attributable to the pensioner or the spouse or common-law partner, was not living with the pensioner in a dwelling maintained by one or the other of them at the time the application was made.

A direction made under subsection (8) in respect of a payment period is deemed to be a direction made in respect of every subsequent payment period, but the Minister may, after any investigation of the circumstances that he or she considers necessary, cancel the direction.

Section.Text: The definition monthly joint income in subsection 22(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: monthly joint income of a pensioner and their spouse or common-law partner in a current payment period is the amount that equals 1/12 of the total of both of their incomes for the base calendar year; however, for the purpose of calculating the allowance that may be paid to the pensioner’s spouse or common-law partner under subsection (3) for either of the following periods, it is the amount that equals 1/12 of the income of the spouse or common-law partner for the base calendar year:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the months during which the pensioner is an incarcerated person described in subsection 5(3), exclusive of the first month of incarceration and the month of release; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a direction made under subsection 19(8) in respect of the spouse or common-law partner has not been cancelled under subsection 19(9), the months, exclusive of the first month, during which the spouse or common-law partner is not living with the pensioner in a dwelling maintained by one or the other of them; (revenu conjoint mensuel)

Section.Text: This Division comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2017.

Subsection.Text: The definition child tax benefit in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Education Savings Act is repealed.
The definition national child benefit supplement in subsection 2(1) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph (a) of the definition primary caregiver in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a beneficiary who is a qualified dependant, the eligible individual in respect of the beneficiary; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Canada child benefit means a deemed overpayment under Subdivision A.1 of Division E of Part I of the Income Tax Act. (allocation canadienne pour enfants)

Subsection.Text: Clause 5(4)(a)(i)(A) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is a qualified dependant of an eligible individual whose adjusted income used to determine the amount of a Canada child benefit in respect of January in the particular year is the first threshold for the particular year or less, or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 5(4)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 10% of the contribution, if the beneficiary is a qualified dependant of an eligible individual whose adjusted income used to determine the amount of a Canada child benefit in respect of January in the particular year is more than the first threshold for the particular year but not more than the second threshold for the particular year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 5(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If there has been no determination of eligibility for a Canada child benefit in respect of January in a particular year, the adjusted income to be used for the purposes of subsection (4) is the adjusted income used to determine the amount of a Canada child benefit for the first month in the particular year in respect of which eligibility has been established.
Subsection 5(6) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Pour l’application du paragraphe (5) au bénéficiaire né en décembre, la mention à ce paragraphe du premier mois de l’année donnée pour lequel le particulier est admissible à l’allocation vaut mention du mois de janvier de l’année suivante.
Subsections 5(6.1) and (6.2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If, in a month following January in a particular year, an individual who was not the primary caregiver of a beneficiary in January of that year becomes the beneficiary’s primary caregiver, then the adjusted income to be used for the purposes of subsection (4) in respect of contributions made to the trustee of the trust designated by that individual is the adjusted income used to determine the amount of a Canada child benefit for the first month in the particular year in respect of which the individual’s eligibility for the Canada child benefit has been established.

In applying subsection (6.1) in respect of a beneficiary for whom the individual becomes the beneficiary’s primary caregiver in December, the reference to “the first month in the particular year in respect of which the individual’s eligibility for the Canada child benefit has been established” in that subsection is to be read as a reference to “January of the next year”.
Paragraphs 6(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $500 in respect of the first benefit year in which the beneficiary is a person less than 15 years of age at the beginning of the month immediately before the benefit year, or is born during the benefit year or during the month immediately before it, and, for at least one month in that year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a qualified dependant of an eligible individual whose adjusted income, when used to determine the amount of a child tax benefit, results in that portion of a child tax benefit determined under the description of C in subsection 122.61(1) of the Income Tax Act being more than $0, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is a person in respect of whom a special allowance under the Children’s Special Allowances Act is payable, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $100 in respect of any subsequent benefit year in which the beneficiary is a person less than 15 years of age at the beginning of the month immediately before the benefit year and, for at least one month in that year, is a person to whom subparagraph (a)(i) or (ii) applies.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 6(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $500 in respect of the first benefit year in which the beneficiary is a person less than 15 years of age at the beginning of the month immediately before the benefit year, or is born during the benefit year or during the month immediately before it, and, for at least one month in that year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a qualified dependant of an eligible individual whose adjusted income used to determine the amount of a Canada child benefit was,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: if the eligible individual has not more than three qualified dependants, less than or equal to the first threshold for the particular year in which the benefit year begins, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: if the eligible individual has more than three qualified dependants, less than the amount determined in accordance with subsection (2.1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is a person in respect of whom a special allowance under the Children’s Special Allowances Act is payable, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $100 in respect of any subsequent benefit year in which the beneficiary is a person less than 15 years of age at the beginning of the month immediately before the benefit year and, for at least one month in that year, is a person to whom subparagraph (a)(i) or (ii) applies.

Subsection.Text: Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):
For greater certainty, a Canada Learning Bond is, other than for the purposes of section 14 of the Canada Education Savings Regulations, not to be paid in respect of any beneficiary

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: more than once in the beneficiary’s lifetime under paragraph 6(2)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: more than once in respect of a benefit year under paragraph 6(2)(b).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 6(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of clause (2)(a)(i)(B), the amount is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined by the formula
is the first threshold for the particular year in which the benefit year begins, and
is the number of qualified dependants in excess of two, and

Subsection.Text: The amounts expressed in dollars in subsection (2.1) are to be adjusted, as set out in section 117.1 of the Income Tax Act, for each particular year after 2016.
The amounts adjusted under subsection (2.2) that are to be used in respect of a benefit year are those amounts so adjusted for the particular year in which that benefit year begins.
A Canada Learning Bond is, other than for the purposes of section 14 of the Canada Education Savings Regulations, not to be paid in respect of any beneficiary

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: more than once in the beneficiary’s lifetime under paragraph 6(2)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: more than once in respect of a benefit year under paragraph 6(2)(b).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 9.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (1), the Minister may not waive any requirement related to the determination of eligibility for a child tax benefit or for a special allowance under the Children’s Special Allowances Act.
Subsection 9.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (1), the Minister may not waive any requirement related to the determination of eligibility for a Canada child benefit or for a special allowance under the Children’s Special Allowances Act.

Section.Text: The heading before section 14 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 14:

Section.MarginalNote: Applications for benefit years before July 1, 2016
Section.Text: The provisions of this Act, of the Income Tax Act and of any regulations made under those Acts, as they read immediately before July 1, 2016, apply to an application for a Canada Learning Bond made in respect of any benefit year, within the meaning of subsection 6(3) of this Act, before that date.

Subsection.Text: Section 14.1 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 14.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:

The provisions of this Act, of the Income Tax Act and of any applicable regulations made under those Acts, as they read immediately before July 1, 2017, apply to an application for a Canada Learning Bond made in respect of the benefit year, within the meaning of subsection 6(3) of this Act, starting on July 1, 2016.
Subsections 107(2) and (3), 109(1) and (3) and 110(1), section 111 and subsection 112(1) are deemed to have come into force on July 1, 2016.
Subsections 107(1) and (4), section 108 and subsections 109(2) and (4), 110(2) and 112(2) come into force on July 1, 2017.
The definition child tax benefit in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Disability Savings Act is repealed.
The definition phase-out income in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: phase-out income for a particular year means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular year is before 2017, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the first threshold for the particular year, and

is the amount referred to in paragraph (a) of the description of F in subsection 122.61(1) of the Income Tax Act, as that subsection read on January 1, 2016, as adjusted under that Act for the particular year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the particular year is 2017 or any subsequent year, the dollar amount referred to in paragraph (a) of the description of Q in subsection 122.61(1) of the Income Tax Act, as adjusted under that Act for the particular year. (revenu de transition)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Canada child benefit means a deemed overpayment under Subdivision A.1 of Division E of Part I of the Income Tax Act. (allocation canadienne pour enfants)

Subsection.Text: The Act is amended by replacing “child tax benefit” with “Canada child benefit” in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph 6(2)(a)(ii) and subsection 6(4); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraphs 7(2)(a)(ii) and (b)(ii) and subsection 7(6).

Subsection.Text: The French version of the Act is amended by replacing “prestation fiscale pour enfants” with “allocation canadienne pour enfants”, with any grammatical changes that the circumstances require, in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 6(5); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 7(7).

Section.Text: This Division comes into force or is deemed to have come into force on January 1, 2017.
Subsection 3(2.1) of the Royal Canadian Mint Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 4(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
In carrying out its objects, the Mint has the rights, powers and privileges and the capacity of a natural person and may if necessary
Subsection 4(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by replacing paragraph (c) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: produce and arrange for the production and supply of coins of the currency of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: produce and arrange for the production of coins of the currency of countries other than Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: make medals, plaques, tokens and other objects;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: melt, assay and refine gold, silver and other metals;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: lease or acquire gold, silver and other metals;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: lend, lease or dispose of gold, silver and other metals;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: issue, promote, deal in or trade in financial services and products relating to gold, silver and other metals, subject to the approval of the Minister and in a manner consistent with the Mint’s last corporate plan as approved under section 122 of the Financial Administration Act;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: securely store and securely ship coins, gold, silver and other metals or arrange for their secure storage and shipment;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: market minting equipment developed by, or on behalf of, the Mint;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: acquire or provide consulting services relating to any activity referred to in paragraphs (c) to (k);

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: obtain, acquire, license, sell, or otherwise make available or dispose of any patent, copyright, industrial design, trademark or other similar property right, or any licence to any of them, relating to any activity referred to in paragraphs (c) to (k);

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: engage in marketing, promotion and research and in development activities relating to any activity referred to in paragraphs (c) to (k);

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: do any of the activities referred to in paragraphs (d) to (f) and (j) on behalf of any person or entity; and

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: subject to the approval of the Minister and in a manner consistent with the Mint’s last corporate plan as approved under section 122 of the Financial Administration Act, engage in any other activity.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 6.3:

Non-circulation coins of the currency of Canada in the denomination of $350 that are dated 1999, 2000, 2001, 2002, 2003, 2004, 2005 or 2006 are current and legal tender in Canada as of the year that they are dated.
Subsection 12(1) of the Act is repealed.
The Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following after section 42.1:

Heading.Label: PART III.2
Heading.TitleText: Financial Transactions Related to Asset Management

Section.Text: The following definitions apply in this Part.

Definition.Text: fiscal agent means a fiscal agent appointed under this Part and includes the Bank of Canada. (agent financier)
registrar means a registrar appointed under this Part and includes the Bank of Canada. (agent comptable)

Subsection.Text: The Minister may, for the sound and efficient management of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, lend money by way of an auction on any terms and conditions that the Minister considers appropriate.
However, the Minister is not to make a loan that exceeds the surplus of the part of the Consolidated Revenue Fund that is on deposit with the Bank of Canada at the time of the making of the loan.
For the purposes of subsection (2), the amount of the surplus is determined by the Bank of Canada.
Any loans made under subsection (1) may only be paid out of the part of the Consolidated Revenue Fund that is on deposit with the Bank of Canada.
Despite section 42.5, the Minister may enter into any contract or agreement related to the loans and do any other thing relating to the loans that the Minister considers appropriate.
If the Minister lends money by way of an auction, the Minister may establish rules governing the conduct of the auction, including rules relating to any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the eligibility of persons to participate in the auction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the provision to the Minister by participants of any information that the Minister considers relevant;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the form of bids;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the maximum amount that a participant may bid.

Subsection.Text: Rules governing the conduct of an auction are not statutory instruments as defined in the Statutory Instruments Act.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may authorize the Minister, subject to any terms and conditions that the Governor in Council may specify, to enter into any contract or agreement of a financial nature, including options, derivatives, swaps and forwards, on any terms and conditions that the Minister considers necessary for the management of risks related to the financial position of the Government of Canada.
The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: appoint one or more registrars or fiscal agents to perform any services that the Minister may specify in respect of financial transactions entered into under this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fix the remuneration or compensation of any registrar or fiscal agent appointed under this section.

Section.Text: With the authorization of the Governor in Council, there may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the remuneration and compensation of registrars and fiscal agents appointed under section 42.6;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all costs, expenses and charges incurred in the management of financial transactions referred to in this Part, including the negotiation, entering into and execution of those transactions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all money required to be paid under contracts and agreements entered into under subsection 42.3(5) or section 42.5;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: all money required to be paid under contracts and agreements entered into under this Act before the coming into force of this Part that could also have been entered into under this Part if this Part had been in force when these contracts and agreements were entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: all money that the Minister considers appropriate to pay in the exercise of his or her power to do any other thing relating to the lending of money under subsection 42.3(5).

Section.Text: The Minister may delegate to any officer of the Department of Finance any of the Minister’s powers, duties or functions under this Part, except the power to delegate under this section.
Section 55 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: all money that the Minister considers appropriate to pay in the exercise of his or her power to do any other thing relating to the borrowing of money under subsection 44(3).

Section.Text: Paragraph 18(m.1) of the Bank of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (m.1)
Paragraph.Text: act as a custodian of the financial assets of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation and of those of the Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation;

Section.Text: Section 24 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: If the Minister may, under any other Act of Parliament that expressly authorizes him or her to do so, lend, at the request of an agent corporation as defined in subsection 83(1) of the Financial Administration Act, money out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to that corporation on any terms and conditions that he or she may fix, the Minister may authorize the Bank to manage the lending of money to that corporation on his or her behalf, on any terms and conditions that he or she may establish.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act

Section.Text: The Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation Act is amended by adding the following after section 34:

Subsection.Text: The Corporation may maintain in its own name one or more accounts with the Bank of Canada.
The Bank of Canada may pay interest on any money that the Corporation deposits with it.

Section.Text: Section 121 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
